name: Transit
content: The bus system is quite good. Downtown Dublin at rush hour (8:30 AM) is the only time I have seen a traffic jam consisting entirely of buses. It is quite a sight to behold: Dublin has one of the densest city centers in Europe, the buses are all double decker (therefore increasing transport density), and at rush hour, the buses are at standing room capacity (pushing density to its absolute limits). Quite frankly, I think Dublin is somewhere near some upper limit of how many humans you can move through an urban area.
